title: Universally optimal distribution of points on spheres

thm:2.summary: Sharp configurations on $S^{n-1}$ (and the regular $600$-cell in $S^3$) are universally optimal: for every completely monotonic $f:(0,4]\to\mathbb{R}$, they minimize $\sum_{x\ne y}f(|x-y|^2)$ among configurations of the same size — the original universal-optimality framework on spheres.

thm:7.summary: Root positivity: for $\alpha\in\mathbb{R}$ and $r_1<\cdots<r_n$ the roots of $p_n+\alpha p_{n-1}$, the partial products $\prod_{i=1}^k(t-r_i)$ (with $k<n$) have positive coefficients in the orthogonal-polynomial basis — the positivity input to the LP bound.

thm:24.summary: Universal optimality on general compact two-point homogeneous spaces $X$: sharp configurations $\mathcal{C}\subset X$ are universally optimal for any absolutely monotonic $f:[-1,1)\to\mathbb{R}$ — the abstract framework generalizing Theorem 2 beyond spheres.

prop:4.summary: Obstruction: for $n+1 < N < 2n$, no $N$-point universally optimal configuration exists on $S^{n-1}$ — a non-existence theorem complementing Theorem 2's existence on the sharp/600-cell sizes.

prop:6.summary: Absolute monotonicity preservation: if $f$ is absolutely monotonic on $(a,b)$, so is $\frac{f(t)-p(t)}{(t-t_1)^{k_1}\cdots(t-t_m)^{k_m}}$ — the algebraic identity letting the LP polynomial be subtracted while preserving monotonicity.

prop:8.summary: Quotient positivity: for the largest root $r$ of $p_n+\alpha p_{n-1}$, the quotient $(p_n+\alpha p_{n-1})/(t-r)$ has positive coefficients in $p_0,\dots,p_{n-1}$ — the local positivity ingredient.

prop:15.summary: Sphere LP bound: for a polynomial $h:[-1,1]\to\mathbb{R}$ with $h(t)\le f(2-2t)$ for $t\in[-1,1)$ and nonnegative coefficients in Gegenbauer polynomials, the energy $\sum f(|x-y|^2)$ is bounded below by an explicit expression — the linear-programming dual on $S^{n-1}$.

prop:25.summary: LP bound on general $X$: for absolutely monotonic $f$ and polynomial $h\le f$ on $[-1,1)$ with nonneg coefficients in the relevant orthogonal polynomials for $X$, the analogous energy bound holds — the abstract LP framework.

prop:31.summary: Euclidean universal-optimality criterion: a Fourier-positive $h:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}$ with $h(x)\le f(|x|^2)$ for $x\ne 0$ gives an energy lower bound for any periodic configuration — the criterion the CKMRV universal-optimality theorem (1902.05438) verifies for $E_8$ and Leech.

conjecture:32.summary: Universal-optimality conjecture for $E_8$ and Leech: for $n\in\{2,8,24\}$ and completely monotonic $f$ with appropriate decay, the corresponding Euclidean auxiliary $h$ exists — the conjecture later proved by CKMRV 2019 (1902.05438:thm:1.4) for $n=8,24$.

prop:34.summary: Existence of Euclidean auxiliary $h$ for completely monotonic $f = O(|x|^{-1/2-\varepsilon})$ at infinity — the asymptotic-decay version of the existence statement.

edges.note: The universal-optimality theorem on $S^{n-1}$ is proved by exhibiting suitable polynomials $h$ for the LP bound (Proposition 15).
The construction uses the absolute-monotonicity preservation lemma (Proposition 6).
Theorem 24 generalizes Theorem 2 from spheres to all compact two-point homogeneous spaces.
The abstract universal-optimality theorem uses the general LP bound (Proposition 25).
The general-$X$ LP bound generalizes the sphere LP bound.
The chosen polynomial $h$ comes from the quotient positivity result (Proposition 8).
Quotient positivity is read off the root positivity theorem (Theorem 7).
The Euclidean criterion is the $\mathbb{R}^n$ analogue of the sphere LP bound; the same Fourier-positive-$h$ mechanism.
Proposition 34 supplies the existence of Euclidean auxiliary $h$ that Proposition 31's criterion requires.
Conjecture 32 predicts that the Euclidean criterion of Proposition 31 is satisfied for $E_8$ and Leech with completely monotonic $f$.

main_results.description: Universal optimality of sharp configurations on spheres and on general compact two-point homogeneous spaces.

LP_bound.description: The linear-programming bounds: sphere version, general two-point homogeneous version, and the Euclidean criterion.

polynomial_positivity.description: Root positivity for $p_n+\alpha p_{n-1}$ and the quotient positivity that constructs the LP polynomial.

preservation_and_existence.description: Absolute-monotonicity preservation under polynomial subtraction and existence of Euclidean auxiliary $h$ with appropriate decay.

obstructions.description: Non-existence of universally optimal configurations at intermediate sizes.

Euclidean_conjecture.description: The conjecture predicting universal optimality of $E_8$ and Leech in the Euclidean setting (later proved by CKMRV 2019).

ConwaySloane.title: Conway–Sloane — Sphere Packings, Lattices and Groups

Levenshtein.title: V. I. Levenshtein — packing bounds via polynomial method
